skill.name: requesting-code-review
skill.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
skill.summary: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requi...
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified external git command examples, but they are part of a legitimate code review workflow. Weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts are false positives based on nearby words, placeholders, and example text. No evidence found of malicious intent, network access, credential access, destructive commands, or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: External Git Commands With Placeholder Ranges
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for the external command findings. The skill instructs the assistant to run git diff and git rev-parse commands with supplied base and head values. This is legitimate for code review, but unsafe placeholder values could cause unintended shell behavior if copied without validation. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The commands are clearly present and semantically relevant, but they are scoped to git review operations and show no malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for weak cryptography and system reconnaissance findings. The cited lines contain metadata, placeholders, review checklist text, example output, or process guidance, not cryptographic code or reconnaissance commands. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: Direct inspection of all cited lines found no weak algorithm implementation, system probing command, secret access, or network behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes bash command examples for resolving commit SHAs and reviewing a diff. These commands should only be run in trusted repositories with validated commit references. Confidence: 0.84. Confidence reasoning: The command examples are explicit, but their purpose is normal git review rather than malware or exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Teams often miss defects when code changes move forward without structured review. This skill provides a repeatable review request workflow with git ranges, severity levels, and merge readiness checks.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to request review after tasks, features, bug fixes, and merge preparation.
Provides git commands for collecting base and head commit references.
Supplies a code-reviewer prompt template with implementation, requirements, and git range fields.
Defines review categories for code quality, architecture, testing, requirements, and production readiness.
Requires findings to include file references, impact, and suggested fixes.
Guides the reviewer to give a clear ready-to-merge assessment.
limitations: Requires accurate commit references and clear requirements from the user.
Depends on the assistant or subagent having access to the repository and diff.
Does not run tests, lint checks, or build commands by itself.
Does not replace human approval for sensitive or regulated code changes.

use_cases.title: Review Completed Feature Work
use_cases.description: Ask for a structured review before a feature branch is merged.

use_cases.title: Check Plan Implementation
use_cases.description: Compare completed tasks against a written plan and identify missing requirements.

use_cases.title: Improve Subagent Workflows
use_cases.description: Insert a review checkpoint after each batch of delegated development tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Review Request
prompt_templates.scenario: A small change is ready for review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my latest change for correctness, test coverage, and merge readiness. Use the code-reviewer format and call out Critical, Important, and Minor issues.

prompt_templates.title: Feature Review
prompt_templates.scenario: A completed feature needs comparison against requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the implemented feature against the requirements I provide. Check architecture, edge cases, tests, documentation, and production readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Diff Range Review
prompt_templates.scenario: A specific git range should be reviewed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the changes between the base and head commits I provide. Focus on functional regressions, security concerns, and missing requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Pre-Merge Gate Review
prompt_templates.scenario: A branch needs a final decision before merge.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a pre-merge review with a clear verdict. Identify blockers, required fixes, optional improvements, and any risks that should be documented.

output_examples.input: Review a completed verification feature before continuing to the next task.
output: Strengths: The implementation has clear structure, meaningful tests, and understandable error handling.
Important: A missing progress indicator may make long operations difficult to monitor.
Assessment: Ready to continue after the important issue is addressed.

output_examples.input: Check whether a feature branch is ready to merge.
output: Critical: No blocking correctness or data loss issues were found.
Important: Add coverage for invalid input and document the new configuration option.
Assessment: Ready to merge with fixes.

best_practices: Use trusted commit references and verify the diff range before review.
Provide the plan or requirements with enough detail for comparison.
Fix Critical and Important issues before continuing development.
anti_patterns: Do not request review without sharing what changed and why.
Do not ignore severe findings because the change appears small.
Do not treat style suggestions as blockers without technical impact.

faq.question: What does this skill help review?
faq.answer: It helps review code changes for quality, architecture, tests, requirements, and production readiness.

faq.question: Does it run the git commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides commands and a review template. The assistant or user decides what to run.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide what was implemented, the requirements or plan, the base commit, and the head commit.

faq.question: Does it replace a human reviewer?
faq.answer: No. It adds a structured AI review checkpoint before human or team approval.

faq.question: What are the main risks?
faq.answer: The main risk is running git commands with untrusted placeholder values. Validate commit references first.
